name: Baumes de Caxurma from Castellfollit de la Roca - Circular through the Natural Park of the volcanic zone of la Garrotxa
name_original: Baumes de Caxurma desde Castellfollit de la Roca - Circular por el Parque Natural de la zona volcánica de la Garrotxa
region_intro: Hiking around Castellfollit De La Roca is characterized by its unique geological formations, including a prominent basaltic cliff where the village is situated. The region is part of the La Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park, featuring numerous volcanic cones and ancient lava flows. Trails often follow the Fluvià and Toronell rivers, providing routes through diverse landscapes of forests and river valleys. This area offers varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Castellfollit De La Roca The most popular hiking route is Baumes de Caxurma from Castellfollit de la Roca - Circular through the Natural Park of the volcanic zone of la Garrotxa, a 6.5 miles (10.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 22 minutes to complete. This moderate circular route explores the volcanic zone. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castellfollit de la Roca – Virgin of the Devesa loop from Castellfollit de la Roca, a moderate 4.8 miles (7.7 km) path. This trail offers views of the surrounding landscape and river valleys. Local hikers also love the Castellfollit de la Roca – Pont Trencat loop from Castellfollit de la Roca, a 3.5 miles (5.6 km) trail leading through the Fluvià river valley and past basaltic formations, often completed in about 1 hour 31 minutes. Hiking around Castellfollit De La Roca is defined by basaltic cliffs, volcanic cones, and river valleys. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging treks. The routes in Castellfollit De La Roca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 800 reviews. More than 5,800 hikers have used komoot to explore Castellfollit De La Roca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What makes hiking in Castellfollit De La Roca unique?
A: Hiking in Castellfollit De La Roca offers a unique experience due to its dramatic geological formations. The village itself is perched atop a towering basaltic cliff, a result of ancient lava flows carved by the Fluvià and Toronell rivers. The region is also a gateway to the La Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park, featuring numerous volcanic cones and diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Castellfollit De La Roca?
A: Yes, there are several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10483912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellfollit de la Roca loop from Castellfollit de la Roca&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3 km route. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17560916" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellfollit de la Roca – Pont Trencat loop from Castellfollit de la Roca&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 5.6 km and takes around 1 hour 30 minutes, leading through the Fluvià river valley.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: You'll encounter a variety of striking natural features. The most prominent is the basaltic cliff of Castellfollit De La Roca itself. Trails often wind through the La Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park, where you can see ancient volcanic cones and lava flows. The Fluvià and Toronell rivers also play a significant role, with routes following their banks through lush forests and river valleys.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular moderate circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937530086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baumes de Caxurma from Castellfollit de la Roca - Circular through the Natural Park of the volcanic zone of la Garrotxa&lt;/a&gt;, which is 10.4 km long. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5334550" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellfollit de la Roca – Virgin of the Devesa loop from Castellfollit de la Roca&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7.7 km path.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see while hiking?
A: Beyond the stunning basalt cliff, you can explore several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2830179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgin of the Devesa&lt;/a&gt; is a notable religious site. You can also discover volcanic features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1134337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Margarida Volcano&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4052842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Croscat Volcano Scoria Slopes&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, there are historical bridges such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2783700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llierca Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2827986" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valentí Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, and even caves like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2830180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baumes de Caixurma&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How many hiking trails are there in Castellfollit De La Roca?
A: There are over 15 hiking trails around Castellfollit De La Roca, offering a range of difficulties from easy strolls to more challenging treks. This includes 7 easy, 8 moderate, and 1 difficult route.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Castellfollit De La Roca?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 885 reviews. Reviewers often praise the unique volcanic landscapes, the dramatic views of the basaltic cliff, and the diverse terrain that includes river valleys and lush forests.

Q: Are there any difficult or challenging hikes in the area?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, there is at least one difficult route. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24569592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgin of the Devesa loop from Castellfollit de la Roca&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 16 km trail that involves significant elevation gain and takes over 5 hours to complete.

Q: Can I explore the village of Castellfollit De La Roca itself as part of a walk?
A: Absolutely. The medieval old town of Castellfollit De La Roca, with its narrow streets built from volcanic rock, is a highlight. You can walk through the village to viewpoints like Josep Pla square, which offers expansive vistas of the river valley and the surrounding landscape. The Church of Sant Salvador also provides historical interest.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Castellfollit De La Roca?
A: The region offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide mild temperatures and vibrant scenery, making them ideal. Summer can be warm, but shaded forest trails offer respite. Winter hiking is also possible, though some higher or more exposed routes might require checking local conditions.

Q: Is there parking available for hikers in Castellfollit De La Roca?
A: Yes, Castellfollit De La Roca typically has designated parking areas for visitors. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for the most convenient parking spots, especially if you plan to start a specific trail from the village.

Q: Are there any routes that follow the rivers Fluvià or Toronell?
A: Many routes in the area incorporate the rivers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17560916" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellfollit de la Roca – Pont Trencat loop from Castellfollit de la Roca&lt;/a&gt;, for example, leads through the Fluvià river valley, offering scenic views along the water and past basaltic formations. These riverine paths often pass by small orchards and historical industrial elements.
tips: Beautiful bridge with a view of the castle. Worth a visit.

The Baumes de Caixurma are a complex system of labyrinthine galleries and narrow cracks that run between large blocks.
Located south of Castellfollit de la Roca, within the municipality of Montagut and Oix.

These are blocks of bluish-gray shale marl. According to the cartography of the Geological Institute of Catalonia, they are Eocene materials from the Lutetian.

The entrances to the baumes, with an approximate length of 400m, are hidden by the dense vegetation

Sanctuary of the Virgen de la Devesa is a building in the municipality of Montagut i Oix (Garrotxa) included in the Inventory of the Architectural Heritage of Catalonia.

It is a small building of Romanesque origin (first documented in 1261), with a rectangular floor plan and a single nave. Its apse is semicircular and the entrance door has an archivolt in relief. It has been reformed on many occasions, the most important of which was in 1540.

Runes of the church of the Virgin of Mont-ros located at the top of Mont-ros, in the northwest buttress of the Sant Julià del Mont mountain range. The first writings in which we find this chapel documented are from 1240.
